TextENGNew: Cooled by the air inside the restaurant, the tension began to drain from me, little by little.
changeReason: Old says 'I was still on edge' but JPN 緊張が覚めてきた means tension began to subside — opposite emotional state described.

TextENGNew: My body ached here and there — probably because I'd been so rigid with nerves.
Shion, now changed back into her uniform, came over carrying two glasses of iced coffee.
"Here, an iced coffee — on the house. Just hand this ticket to the register and it won't cost you a thing."

...I was sorry for everything, truly....
But since she'd gone to the trouble, I decided to accept.
changeReason: Old says 'My patheticness really knew no bounds' (self-deprecation about being pathetic); new says 'I was sorry for everything, truly' (expressing gratitude/apology). TextJPN 何から何まで本当に申し訳ない means 'truly sorry for everything.'

TextENGNew:
It had been such a good day, too... and then I had to go and get carried away like that.
...I'd completely ruined it.
"But seriously... you really saved me back there. You looked genuinely dependable, Shion."
changeReason: Old says 'I've really come to see you as reliable' (ongoing realization); new says 'You looked genuinely dependable, Shion' (specific moment observation). TextJPN 詩音が本当に頼もしく見えた means 'Shion genuinely looked dependable' (past tense, specific moment).

TextENGNew: "I was scared too, you know. But it was Kei-chan in trouble, so..."
"I worked up my courage and did what I had to do."
Saying that, she blushed ever so slightly and flexed her arm for show.
changeReason: Old says 'made a show of folding her arms boldly'; new says 'blushed ever so slightly and flexed her arm for show.' TextJPN 腕をぐっと立てて見せた means showing off by flexing/raising an arm, not folding arms.

TextENGNew: "Shion... thank you, really."

...I was saying thank you to Shion.
...But it was Mion I really wanted to thank.
...Mion, thank you, truly...

"Now, now, cheer up already. If you feel like you owe someone, then next time you can fight for somebody else.
...Like, if I'm ever in a pinch —
you'll come save me, right?"
"Ah... yeah, of course. I promise."
"Ahahaha! You absolutely have to!"
Shion latched on to my promise like she'd been waiting for exactly those words, and beamed with delight.
changeReason: Old says 'Taking advantage of my words, she seemed happy'; new says 'Shion latched on to my promise like she'd been waiting for exactly those words, and beamed with delight.' New adds interpretive detail about her anticipation.

TextENGNew:
"Kei-chan will come save me when I'm in trouble~!
I can't wait to be in trouble~☆ ..........Ah — you finally smiled."
Saying that, Shion smiled and poked me in the cheek.
"A glum Kei-chan is basically an imposter. Come on, smile! Smile!"
"Th-That tickles...! St-stop it, wahahahahaha...!!"
With Shion poking and prodding at me, I finally started to feel myself again.
The muscles that had been locked tight ever since that incident finally started to loosen...
"When you start feeling better, don't you get hungry? I'm starving — I want some pancakes or something. Do you want some too, Kei-chan?"

I thought about holding back, but holding back wasn't really my style.
So I didn't.
"They're good here, right? I'm pretty particular about my pancakes."
"Ahahaha! Don't worry — our pancakes are quite well-regarded."
Shion flagged down a passing waitress and put in an order for pancakes.
There was something a little strange about a staff member ordering from another staff member...
"...Hey, wait — aren't you in the middle of your shift, Shion? Is it really okay to just sit here chatting with me?"

``I told my uncle that Kei-chan saved me from some delinquents, so he said to treat you right☆
The manager gave his blessing, so it's fine.``
changeReason: Old says 'you saved me when I was surrounded by hoodlums, so he's rewarding you'; new says 'Kei-chan saved me from some delinquents, so he said to treat you right.' Old says the uncle is rewarding Keiichi; new says the uncle told Shion to treat Keiichi well. TextJPN ねぎらってあげろ means 'show appreciation/treat him well.'

TextENGNew: "Th-That's the exact opposite of what happened... Is that really okay...!?"
"It's perfectly fine. ...Ahahahahahahaha!"
By the time the pancakes arrived, the air between us had softened completely and conversation flowed easily.
The two of us ate pancakes and let the talk wander wherever it liked.
...TV shows, celebrities, that sort of thing.

Hobbies, food.
Just idle, throwaway talk.
Talking like this, I found myself thinking once more — Shion really is a girl, isn't she.
...Hold on, that's a strange thought to have, Keiichi Maebara.
Shion and Mion are the same person.
If there was any difference at all... it was only whether Mion was willing to acknowledge herself as Mion.
And yet — why did our conversations feel so different?
"Kei-chan, you don't really think of my sister as a girl, do you~."
"Th-That's not true. If she were a guy I'd have knocked her flat by now!"
changeReason: Old says 'If she were a guy I'd punch the living daylights out of her'; new says 'If she were a guy I'd have knocked her flat by now.' Different levels of violence described, and tense differs (conditional vs past conditional).

TextENGNew:
"I think you and my sister get along so well because there's nothing romantic between you — it's purely platonic.
...That's actually a pretty rare kind of relationship. Enviable, even."
"...I'm not totally sure what you mean, but... yeah, she's honestly too good to be confined to just being a girl."
changeReason: Old says 'it really would be a waste to treat her like a girl'; new says 'she's honestly too good to be confined to just being a girl.' TextJPN 女にしとくにはもったいない means 'it's a waste to keep her as just a girl,' and the new translation captures the 'confined' nuance differently.

TextENGNew: "Heeey, Kei-chan, that's sexist! Ahahahahaha!"
The word "sexist" gave me pause, and I looked inward.
Just what kind of prejudice did I hold toward Mion and Shion?
Mion was Mion.
She was my greatest friend, the kind you didn't need to stand on ceremony with — and always three steps ahead of me as club leader.
changeReason: Old says 'head and shoulders above me when it came to club activities'; new says 'always three steps ahead of me as club leader.' TextJPN says ３枚は上手 (three levels above), and 'head and shoulders' vs 'three steps ahead' convey different magnitudes.

TextENGNew: If I'd met her five years sooner, I had no doubt my life would have been all the richer for it.
Then what about Shion?

...She was Mion's little sister, and it hadn't even been three days since we met.
Our only tie was that indirect one — I knew her older sister. That made her far more of a stranger than Mion.
And for Shion, the same was true — from her side, I was nothing more than a friend of her sister's whom she'd met less than three days ago.

...The difference between Mion and Shion?
...Closeness?
...Familiarity?
...I was tossed about by thoughts I couldn't quite pin down, and for a while, Shion's voice didn't reach me at all.
